title: NOC 44100 – Home Child Care Providers | GTR Immigration
description: Home Child Care Providers under NOC 44100 provide care and supervision for children in private households. Classified under TEER 4, this occupation plays an essential role in supporting working families and early childhood development across Canada. These professionals may work:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER4 – Occupations usually require a secondary school diploma or several weeks of on-the-job trainingMajor Group44 – Care providers and public protection support occupationsSub-Major Group441 – Home care provider occupationsMinor Group4410 – Home care provider occupationsNOC Code44100

duties: Supervise and care for children in the employer’s or own residence
Bathe, dress, and feed infants and children
Prepare infant formulas and change diapers
Maintain a safe and healthy home environment
Transport children to school and appointments
Maintain daily activity and health records
Work under guidance of foster parent agencies
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school may be required
Training in child care or related field may be required
Experience in child care or household management may be required
First aid and CPR certification may be required
Demonstrated ability to care for children
Employers may require background checks and references.
salary_prospects: Home Child Care Providers typically earn:
$25,100 – $44,500 per year,depending on province, experience, and working arrangements.
job_outlook: Home Child Care Providers may qualify under specific caregiver immigration pathways, subject to eligibility criteria including:
Immigration programs for caregivers are subject to policy updates and caps.
If you are planning to work in Canada as a Home Child Care Provider, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your pathway to Canada PR under NOC 44100.
labour_market_demand: Home Child Care Providers are in strong demand due to:
Demand is steady across major provinces including Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Quebec.
Opportunities in private households and agencies
Pathways to permanent residence through caregiver programs
Home Child Care Providers play a vital role in child development and family support systems.
